hero.title: Flag Day
hero.reason: In honor of Flag Day, we honor the American spirit with this drama film about a complex family dynamic, starring Sean Penn and his son.
hero.blurb: Flag Day is a poignant drama that weaves together themes of love, family, and patriotism. With powerful performances and beautiful cinematography, it's a must-watch for anyone looking for a thoughtful, character-driven film.

released.title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer is an action-packed superhero sequel that still delights with its thrilling storyline and impressive visual effects.
released.blurb: The Fantastic Four are back and better than ever in this exciting sequel, which introduces the iconic Silver Surfer. With heart-pumping action sequences and stunning visual effects, it's a fun, family-friendly film that's perfect for superhero fans.

birthday.title: The Devil's Advocate
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Donald Sutherland, born June 17 is incorrect since today is June 14, however, Donald Sutherland was in this film, but the actual birthday person is not in our database. Instead, we have Donald Sutherland: The film stars Al Pacino and Keanu Reeves, with Donald Sutherland in a minor role.
birthday.blurb: The Devil's Advocate is a thought-provoking thriller that explores the darker side of human nature. With outstanding performances from Al Pacino and Keanu Reeves, it's a gripping film that will keep you on the edge of your seat.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: This psychological thriller has become infamous for its convoluted plot, poor acting, and cringe-worthy dialogue, making it a prime candidate for a dumpster dive.
awful.blurb: I Know Who Killed Me is a mind-bogglingly bad film that will leave you scratching your head in confusion. With a nonsensical storyline and atrocious acting, it's a trainwreck that's so bad it's almost good.
